article_title: Bun's Zig-to-Rust Rewrite Lands on Main in a Single Million-Line PR Authored Almost Entirely by Claude
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.details:
devclass.com: https://www.devclass.com/software/2026/05/11/anthrophics-bun-team-trials-port-from-zig-to-rust/5237835
kristoff.it: https://kristoff.it/blog/contributor-poker-and-ai/
byteiota.com: https://byteiota.com/bun-rust-rewrite-merged-the-13000-unsafe-block-problem/
simonwillison.net: https://simonwillison.net/2026/Apr/30/zig-anti-ai/
en.wikipedia.org: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bun_%28software%29
lilting.ch: https://lilting.ch/en/articles/bun-zig-rust-ai-port

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong Analysis-category coverage with exemplary Rule 9 discipline (PR #30412, PR #30680, Bun v1.3.14 release page, Stenberg blog, Cro blog all cited as primary publications). The PR-level facts (1,009,257 / 4,024 / 6,755 / 2,188 for #30412; 22 / 639,678 / 1,236 for #30680; 'Rewrite Bun in Rust' / 'ai slop' titles; Jarred-Sumner as author + merger; 2026-05-14T08:09:34Z merge timestamp) are not in the GitHub HTML snapshots (they render via JS) but verified directly via the GitHub API. All quotes traced verbatim: Sumner 'pre-existing test suite' / '3 MB - 8 MB' / 'last version in Zig' / 'this is already the status quo' (PR + Register); Sumner DevClass 'haven't committed to rewriting' + 'no human contribution allowed'; Cro 'contributor poker' / 'play the person, not the cards' / 'mostly negative' / 'insane 10 thousand line long first time PRs' (kristoff.it); Willison 'No LLMs for issues. No LLMs for pull requests. No LLMs for comments on the bug tracker.'; byteiota's 13,000 unsafe blocks + 681,000 Rust LOC + 99.8% test pass + 16,000 compiler errors + 73 unsafe / 350,000 LOC uv comparison; lilting.ch branch names `claude/phase-a-port`, `claude/bench-until-green`, `claude/code-dedup`. One minor paraphrase-in-quote-marks issue: 'cannot be merged upstream' inside quote marks attributed to DevClass; DevClass actually says 'cannot be upstreamed to Zig.' Filed correction.

source-0.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): the rewrite PR itself. Title 'Rewrite Bun in Rust' and Sumner's 'pre-existing test suite' / '3 MB - 8 MB' description verified in plain text. PR counts (1,009,257 / 4,024 / 6,755 / 2,188) verified via GitHub API: additions=1009257, deletions=4024, commits=6755, changed_files=2188, merged_at=2026-05-14T08:09:34Z, merged_by=Jarred-Sumner.

source-1.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): the 'ai slop' cleanup PR. Title 'ai slop' verified in plain text. Counts (22 / 639,678 / 1,236) verified via GitHub API: additions=22, deletions=639678, changed_files=1236, state=closed.

source-2.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): Bun v1.3.14 release. Verifies the 'last Zig-built version' framing.

source-3.status: The Register. Verifies 'shrinks the binary size between 3 and 8 MB', 'last version in Zig' Sumner quote, 'this is already the status quo; we haven't been typing code ourselves for many months now' Sumner quote, and GitHub auto-flagging anecdote.

source-4.status: DevClass. Verifies 'we haven't committed to rewriting' Sumner quote verbatim, four-times debug-compilation framing, OSS opposite-direction quote, Anthropic-acquired-and-uses-it-for-Claude-Code framing. NOTE: article attributes 'cannot be merged upstream' to DevClass in quote marks; the source actually says 'cannot be upstreamed to Zig'. Paraphrase in quote marks — filed correction.

source-5.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): Loris Cro blog post. Verifies all Cro quotes verbatim — 'contributor poker', 'play the person, not the cards', 'mostly negative', 'insane 10 thousand line long first time PRs'.

source-6.status: byteiota. Verifies 13,000+ unsafe blocks, 681,000 Rust LOC, 99.8% test pass on Linux x86-64 glibc, 16,000 compiler errors at start, 73-unsafe-blocks / 350,000 LOC uv comparison.

source-7.status: Simon Willison. Verifies Zig 'No LLMs for issues. No LLMs for pull requests. No LLMs for comments on the bug tracker.' summary verbatim.

source-8.status: Wikipedia. Verifies JavaScriptCore embedding and Anthropic December 2025 acquisition framing.

editor_notes.content_quality: Analysis-category (~1,400 words, within 800–2000). Overview / What Was Merged / How It Was Built / The Zig Backstory / The Unsafe-Block Problem / What's Open structure. Genuinely strong analytical contribution: ties the Zig-no-AI policy frame to Bun's exit move, contrasts the 13,000-unsafe-block density against uv's 73, and surfaces both Sumner's optimism ('OSS to go the opposite direction') and Cro's contributor-poker critique in their own words. Neutral framing.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the May 14 Bun Rust rewrite merge. Builds on prior Bun memory-fixes coverage (linked internally to v1.1.13 article) without duplicating it.
concerns: Paraphrase in quote marks: 'cannot be merged upstream' attributed to DevClass, but DevClass's actual phrasing is 'cannot be upstreamed to Zig'. Same meaning; the quote marks should have wrapped the source's exact wording.
recommendations: Add devclass.com, kristoff.it, byteiota.com, simonwillison.net, en.wikipedia.org, lilting.ch to config/source_allowlist.txt — devclass and simonwillison in particular will recur frequently in developer-tooling coverage.

content_preview.title: Bun's Zig-to-Rust Rewrite Lands on Main in a Single Million-Line PR Authored Almost Entirely by Claude
content_preview.summary: Jarred Sumner merged PR #30412 'Rewrite Bun in Rust' into the oven-sh/bun main branch on May 14, adding 1,009,257 lines of Rust across 6,755 commits over roughly nine days, with the work largely generated by Anthropic's Claude. The merge follows Bun v1.3.14, the runtime's last release in Zig.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Bun creator Jarred Sumner merged [PR #30412 "Rewrite Bun in Rust"](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/pull/30412) into the `oven-sh/bun` main branch on May 14, 2026, replacing the JavaScript runtime's Zig core with a Rust port that was, by Sumner's own account, produced by Anthropic's Claude with minimal human typing. The pull request adds 1,009,257 lines, removes 4,024, and spans 6,755 commits across 2,188 files, [according to the PR metadata on GitHub](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/p...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
